title: Seeing Old Florida in a New Light: The Art of A.E. Backus and the Florida Highwaymen
shortWhy: Seeing Old Florida in a New Light live at Coral Gables Museum.
editorialWhy: For more than a century, artists have gathered along the Indian River to build a powerful tradition of landscape painting that continues to resonate today. Chief among them is A.E. “Beanie” Backus — the 20th century’s foremost painter of the Florida landscape — whose generosity of spirit helped shape one of American art’s most remarkable stories, the legend of the painters known today  At Coral Gables Museum in Coral Gables.

title: Liberté 2026 International Neurodivergent Art & Poetry Contest Opening Night and Award Ceremony
shortWhy: Culture at Coral Gables Museum, Coral Gables.
editorialWhy: Liberté 2026 is an international art and poetry contest open to neurodivergent artists worldwide. Participants are invited to submit one visual artwork or one poem responding to this year’s theme, “I Have a Dream…”. Selected works will be exhibited at ArtLabbé galleries, and winners across three age categories will receive a cash prize. Registration is free and open to all eligible participants. At Coral Gables Museum in Coral Gables.
